title: IMO Approves Net-zero Regulations for Global Shipping
content: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has approved draft regulations to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions from ships globally, aiming for net-zero emissions by or around, i.e close to 2050. It will be formally adopted in October before coming into force in 2027. Approved by the Marine Environment Protection Committee during its 83rd session (MEPC 83) from 7â€“11 April 2025, the measures include a new fuel standard for ships and a global pricing mechanism for emissions.
author: Analysts Account
